activities_projects.title: Theme and Message Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand the deeper theme of resilience and the strength of nature
steps: Read the poem carefully
Identify lines showing how difficult it is to destroy a tree
Write 3–4 sentences explaining the poet’s message about nature and patience
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops comprehension and thematic analysis skills

activities_projects.title: Literary Device Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Identify figurative language and its impact
steps: Find examples of imagery, repetition, and personification
Explain how these devices emphasize the tree’s strength and resilience
Write a short paragraph analyzing their effect
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes poetic techniques and their impact

activities_projects.title: Cause and Effect Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand consequences of human actions on nature
steps: Identify lines showing what happens when a tree is cut improperly
Explain why simply hacking the tree is not enough
Write a paragraph connecting the poem to environmental awareness
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops analytical thinking and moral reasoning

activities_projects.title: Character and Perspective Study
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the poet’s viewpoint towards humans and nature
steps: Explain the poet’s perspective on trees and human interference
Write 3–4 sentences on what the poet wants people to learn from nature
Provide textual references
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Enhances critical thinking and understanding of author’s perspective
activities_projects.evaluation: Textual support and logical explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary Expansion
activities_projects.objective: Learn advanced words and their meanings
steps: Find words like leprous, anchoring, scorching, withering
Write meanings and synonyms
Use these words in sentences related to the poem

activities_projects.title: Comparative Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Compare poem with real-life environmental examples
steps: Think of a situation where a tree or forest was destroyed or protected
Compare the poem’s message with real-life consequences
Write 3–4 sentences connecting the poem to human responsibility

activities_projects.title: Critical Discussion
activities_projects.objective: Engage in class discussion about nature and human actions
steps: Discuss why humans need patience and respect for nature
Write 2–3 key points from the discussion
Explain your own viewpoint with reasoning
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops reasoning, discussion, and critical thinking skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and clarity of ideas (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Reflective Writing
activities_projects.objective: Express personal thoughts inspired by the poem
steps: Write 5–6 sentences about what you can do to protect trees in your neighborhood
Include actions and reflections inspired by the poem
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops reflection and personal connection to literature

activities_projects.title: Poetic Tone Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Identify the tone and mood of the poem
steps: Identify lines that express warning, respect, or reflection
Write 3–4 sentences describing the tone
Explain how tone supports the poem’s message
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Enhances understanding of tone and mood in poetry

activities_projects.title: Creative Illustration
steps: Draw a strong tree with roots and leaves, showing resilience
Label your illustration to highlight strength and survival
Share your drawing in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops creativity and understanding of the poem’s message
